claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child (or children).

**First question: How old is your child?**

The age of your child determines which eligibility path we'll follow:
- **Under 16**: Generally eligible (with some conditions)
- **16 to 19**: May be eligible if in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is between 16 and 19 years old, eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether they are in approved education or training. Let me navigate to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex currently in education or training?**

For 16-19 year olds, there are different situations:

1. **Currently in approved education or training** - This would be full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or approved training programs)

2. **Recently left education or training** - Left within the last 20 weeks (there may be a temporary extension period)

3. **Not in education/training and hasn't recently left**

Which situation applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They left school a while back and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex left school a while back and isn't currently in education or training, I need to check one more thing:

**How long ago did Alex leave education or training?**

Specifically, did Alex leave within the **last 20 weeks** (approximately 5 months)? 

If it's been longer than 20 weeks since Alex left education or training, then unfortunately Alex would no longer be considered a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes, as the benefit for 16-19 year olds only continues while they're in approved education/training or for a limited extension period after leaving.

Can you tell me approximately when Alex left school or any education/training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been quite a bit longer than that - Alex left school last year, so it's been well over 5 months now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me process this through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

## Why you're not eligible:

For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit only continues if they are in **approved full-time non-advanced education or training** (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or approved training programs).

Since Alex:
- Is 17 years old
- Is not currently in education or training
- Left school over a year ago (well beyond the 20-week extension period)

Alex does not meet the qualifying child criteria under the Child Benefit rules.

## What you should know:

- If Alex had left education or training within the last 20 weeks (approximately 5 months), there might have been a temporary extension period available
- Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds is specifically designed to support young people who are continuing their education or training

## Possible next steps:

If Alex decides to return to approved full-time education or training in the future, you may become eligible for Child Benefit again. This would include courses like:
- A-levels or Scottish Highers
- NVQs or other vocational qualifications (up to level 3)
- Approved training programs

If you have any questions or if Alex's circumstances change, you can contact HMRC or visit the official Child Benefit guidance for more information.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which falls in the 16-19 age category. For children aged 16-19, they must be in approved full-time non-advanced education or training to qualify for Child Benefit. Alex is not currently in education or training and left school over a year ago, well beyond the 20-week extension period that applies after leaving education. Therefore, Alex does not meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who is not currently in approved education or training and has been out of education for over a year. Since Alex exceeds the 20-week extension period after leaving education, they do not qualify as a qualifying child under the Child Benefit rules for 16-19 year olds.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.
